chapter_title.en: The Obligation of the jumu’ah Prayer, Its Virtue, Who is Exempt From it, and the Prayer and Sermon Therein

text: 1257 - وَ كَانَ رَسُولُ اَللَّهِ صَلَّى اَللَّهُ عَلَيْهِ وَ آلِهِ إِذَا كَانَ يَوْمُ اَلْجُمُعَةِ وَ لَمْ يُصِبْ طِيباً دَعَا بِثَوْبٍ مَصْبُوغٍ بِزَعْفَرَانٍ فَرَشَّ عَلَيْهِ اَلْمَاءَ ثُمَّ مَسَحَ بِيَدِهِ ثُمَّ مَسَحَ بِهِ وَجْهَهُ.

en.bab-ul-qaim-publications: Hadith.1257 - The Messenger of Allah (sw), when Jumu’ah arrived and he did not have any perfume, would request a cloth dyed with saffron, sprinkle water on it, then rub his hands on it, and then wipe it over his face.
